draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer fluid, animated list transitions over instant refreshes when filtering or sorting contacts
facade.label: List slides in smoothly
facade.acceptImplies: CRM should use easing curves and staggered reveals for all data state changes to feel premium and intentional
facade.rejectImplies: CRM should load and switch views instantly with no motion, prioritizing speed and directness over visual polish

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond positively to interfaces with warm, inviting color palettes and soft lighting that reduce visual fatigue during long work sessions
facade.label: Warm glow
facade.acceptImplies: Explore further with amber, gold, or warm neutral color schemes; test soft shadows and candlelight-inspired interfaces
facade.rejectImplies: Pivot toward cooler palettes, higher contrast, or neutral grays; freelancers may prefer clinical clarity over coziness

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer fluid, animated list transitions over instant refreshes when filtering or sorting contacts
evidence.implication: CRM should use easing curves and staggered reveals for all data state changes to feel premium and intentional

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer a card-based overview of active clients over a dense contact table
facade.axisTargeted: dense data vs key metrics — whether to show all client fields at once or surface 3-5 essential details per client
facade.label: Client list as cards
facade.acceptImplies: visual scanability and quick status checks matter more than comprehensive data density in the primary view
facade.rejectImplies: freelancers need to see all client info (rates, last contact, project count, payment terms) in one glance without clicking into records

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to a CRM that feels like a trusted colleague rather than an enterprise system
facade.label: Warm, personal tone
facade.acceptImplies: Explore conversational copy, emoji accents, informal language, human-centered microcopy that builds rapport
facade.rejectImplies: Test austere, corporate language—clean terminology, formal address, confidence-focused messaging that emphasizes control and metrics

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to calming, uncluttered design that reduces cognitive load when managing multiple clients
facade.axisTargeted: calm vs energetic + organic vs digital
facade.label: Soft, minimal interface
facade.acceptImplies: user values serenity and simplicity; probe next toward warmth and intimacy in interactions
facade.rejectImplies: user may prefer more structure, visual hierarchy, or dynamic feedback; explore energetic or crisp design directions

facade.hypothesis: A CRM for freelancers benefits from a persistent navigation sidebar paired with a main canvas showing clients as individual cards rather than a dense table—balancing quick filtering with readable client summaries.
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure + information density (sidebar vs. tabs; cards vs. tables)
facade.label: Sidebar with card list
facade.acceptImplies: User values persistent context and scannable summaries; likely prefers to keep navigation visible and information chunked visually.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer tab-based navigation or table layouts for dense data; values space efficiency or rapid column-based scanning.

axes.label: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels energetic and branded
Whether they favor dense information dashboards vs. progressive disclosure—reveals if they want everything visible at once or prefer guided, step-by-step navigation
Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual/supportive tone—reveals if they want business-like efficiency or human encouragement
Whether they prefer calm, minimal atmosphere vs. energetic, motivational atmosphere—reveals if they want zen focus or inspiring momentum
Whether they want data-heavy reporting and metrics vs. simple, narrative summaries—reveals if they're metrics-driven or prefer storytelling about their work
Whether they prefer instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, considered transitions—reveals if they want speed and directness or polish and flow

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels energetic and branded
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they favor dense information dashboards vs. progressive disclosure—reveals if they want everything visible at once or prefer guided, step-by-step navigation
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual/supportive tone—reveals if they want business-like efficiency or human encouragement
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer calm, minimal atmosphere vs. energetic, motivational atmosphere—reveals if they want zen focus or inspiring momentum
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they want data-heavy reporting and metrics vs. simple, narrative summaries—reveals if they're metrics-driven or prefer storytelling about their work
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, considered transitions—reveals if they want speed and directness or polish and flow
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer direct manipulation (dragging contacts/projects) over menu-based reorganization, as it feels more responsive and gives immediate visual feedback during workflow management
facade.axisTargeted: MOTION AND BEHAVIOR — gestural interaction vs menu-driven commands
facade.label: Drag to reorder
facade.acceptImplies: CRM should support drag-and-drop for contacts, project grouping, and priority ranking with smooth animations that signal progress mid-gesture
facade.rejectImplies: Freelancers prioritize speed and efficiency over tactile satisfaction; CRM should use fast, lightweight interactions (checkboxes, dropdowns) without animation overhead
